reason: Authoritative spec for how each platform (Gemini, Codex, Claude...) is wired — agent/skill/hook directory layouts, configurator patterns, .agents/skills/ shared-source convention. Implementer must match these contracts.
Spec for .gemini/, .codex/, .agents/ directory shapes and which configurator owns which paths. Needed when redirecting Gemini skills from .gemini/skills/ to .agents/skills/.
Defines the per-turn breadcrumb contract injected by inject-workflow-state.py — output JSON shape, hookEventName field semantics. Implementer changes the platform branch here.
Conventions for shared-hooks Python scripts (CWD-robust root discovery, silent exit, JSON-only stdout). Implementer edits inject-workflow-state.py and must keep these invariants.
Standards for configurator changes: parallel collectTemplates() + configure() updates, lint/type rules, no premature abstractions.
Decision basis for omitting `tools:` line; covers Gemini tool-name slug mismatches the implementer must NOT try to map.
Drop-in JSON snippet for .gemini/settings.json + the matcher/payload contract for BeforeAgent (vs SessionStart).
Confirms .agents/skills/ precedence + frontmatter compatibility — basis for switching Gemini skills target without extra adapter logic.
Locked decisions, two-commit plan, and exact file list to edit.
